2000-06-01.title: News of North Korean's Visit to China Seeps Out
2000-06-01.abstract: China informs South Korea and United States that North Korean leader Kim Jong Il made secret visit to Beijing this week to consult China on coming summit meeting between North and South Korea (M)

2000-06-01.title: World Briefing
2000-06-01.abstract: EUROPE    NORTHERN IRELAND: RESHAPING POLICE  Tom Constantine, 61, a former New York State police chief and head of the Drug Enforcement Administration until his retirement last year, is to take charge of reshaping Northern Ireland's police. The British government said he would be responsible for overseeing proposed reforms to the Royal Ulster Constabulary, the 93-percent Protestant force that is to be renamed the Police Service of Northern Ireland and employ more Catholics.    Warren Hoge (NYT)

2000-06-01.title: A Wall on Trade Between U.S. and Europe
2000-06-01.abstract: Pres Clinton, visiting Europe, meets with European Union leaders Romano Prodi and Prime Min Antonio Guterres, Lisbon, to discuss four major trade disputes; they fail to make any progress and devote bulk of meeting to other issues (M)

2000-06-01.title: U.N. Says Syrians Agree That Israel Is Out of Lebanon
2000-06-01.abstract: United Nations envoy Terje Roed-Larsen, after meeting with Min Farouk al-Shara, says Syria is ready to accept Israel's retreat from southern Lebanon as complete withdrawal, move that could lessen possibility of violence along Israeli-Lebanese border; says Syria agrees with UN report that Israel does not have to give up Shabaa Farms, some 10 square miles of contested land, to comply with Security Council resolution, which dealt only with land involved in Israel's 1982 invasion of Lebanon; Israel seized most of Shabaa Farms in 1967; reaction in Lebanon is confused; Hezbollah militia and government have threatened to continue attacking Israelis if land is not returned; map; photo (M)

2000-06-01.title: Hyundai Chief Quits to Allow Restructuring
2000-06-01.abstract: On the 15th floor of his headquarters in downtown Seoul, Chung Ju Yung, the 84-year-old honorary chairman of Hyundai Group, announced the unthinkable Wednesday: He and the two sons he had chosen to take over most of the sprawling but troubled industrial empire would resign to make way for professional management.

2000-06-02.title: Home Is Still a Memory For Multitude in Eritrea
2000-06-02.abstract: Relief officials say it is far from certain that hundreds of thousands of Eritreans, displaced during three weeks of intense fighting with Ethiopia, will return home anytime soon even though both countries seem to have finally made progress toward ending two-year war; say many people will be too afraid to return; worry that crisis will make Eritrea's already bad food shortages far worse; map; photo (M)

2000-06-02.title: Factory Turnaround Reflects Economic Glimmer in Russia
2000-06-02.abstract: Russia's economy shows signs of resurgence, with industrial production through April up 10.3 percent over same period in 1999; exports jumped 50 percent, and average worker's salary increased 7 percent in March over year ago; companies and government have started paying workers months and even years of back wages; inflation in current year is expected to run about 13 percent, or far below 1999 rate, and budget now runs a primary surplus; Prime Min Vladimir Putin warns growth cannot last without basic economic reforms; already has proposed wholesale changes in taxes, including flat income tax and corporate tax cuts, that appear to have strong support despite concerns that wealthy will benefit most; Eduard F Baranov, first deputy chief of government's Economic Research Center, and other Russian officials comment; photo; map; graph (M)

2000-06-02.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-06-02.abstract: EUROPE    LUXEMBOURG: HOSTAGE-TAKER SHOT -- Police officers used a gun disguised as a television camera and the lure of an interview to shoot a gunman, critically injuring him and freeing 25 children and 3 adults who had been held hostage for more than 28 hours at a day-care center. None of the hostages were hurt. The hostage-taker, left, who had demanded to be flown to Libya, was armed with a pistol, grenades and a knife.    (Reuters)    BRITAIN: LONDON BOMB BLAST -- A small bomb blew up under the Hammersmith Bridge over the Thames , causing structural damage and traffic chaos but no injuries. No one claimed responsibility, but authorities speculated that the blast was timed by dissident Irish republicans to disrupt the power-sharing Northern Ireland government. The bridge has been the target of two prior Irish republican bombs.    Warren Hoge (NYT)

2000-06-02.title: Clinton Finds Germans Critical of U.S. Missile Defense Plan
2000-06-02.abstract: Pres Clinton arrives in Germany for three-day visit; faces immediate criticism for administration's plans to deploy limited missile defense system; meets with Chancellor Gerhard Schroder, who voices concern that development of system may trigger new arms race; other German officials warn system could weaken military ties between US and European allies; accuse US of overstating threat; fears of other European leaders that system could upset existing disarmament accords noted (M)

2000-06-02.title: Moscow Foresees a Balanced Budget
2000-06-02.abstract: Prime Min Mikhail Kasaynov tells cabinet Russia's next federal budget should be balanced for first time in government's 10-year history, even allowing for sizable increase in social spending (S)

2000-06-03.title: World Briefing
2000-06-03.abstract: EUROPE    GREECE: TURKS STORM BEACH -- In a dramatic display of the improved state of Greek-Turkish relations, about 160 Turkish troops, one of whom is at left, stormed a beach at Kyparissia, in the western Peloponnesus, as part of a NATO exercise in recapturing lost territory. Some 2,000 Greek, American and Spanish marines were also involved. Greece and Turkey came close to war in 1996 when Turks seized a disputed rocky islet about 310 miles west of Kyparissia.   Anthee Carassava (NYT)

2000-06-03.title: PUTIN SUGGESTING ALTERNATIVE PLAN ON MISSILE DEFENSE
2000-06-03.abstract: Pres Vladimir V Putin hints that Russia and US could collaborate on new ways to shoot down enemy missiles soon after they are launched, rather than in space, as proposed by US; idea seems to resemble plan known in US as 'boost phase defense,' which has been proposed by a number of arms control advocates, who say it would provide US, Europe and Russia with the protection that Clinton administration insists it needs from so-called rogue states; such a system would be little use against Russian nuclear force, making it far more acceptable to Russian military; administration officials react cautiously to suggestion (M)

2000-06-03.title: Indonesia's Uphill War on Rule From the Top
2000-06-03.abstract: Indonesia clamors for democracy but is directed from the top by Pres Abdurrahman Wahid much as it was under Suharto until he was forced out in May 1998; Wahid's ability to enact his policies is dangerously limited, however, because Suharto eviscerated the nation's institutions; he rules through the force of his personality, through his moral authority, through the weakness of his rivals and through a sort of lighthearted bullying, but he can never be certain how his directives will be carried out; photo (M)

2000-06-03.title: Rome Journal; Dueling Festivals: Gay Pride and Vatican Collide
2000-06-03.abstract: Italian politics are roiled in wake of Vatican's effort to block gay pride festival in Rome, center of Roman Catholic Church's Holy Year festivities; Mayor Francesco Rutelli withdraw's city's support for event, which he first welcomed, drawing fire from his center-left constituency without appeasing the church; photo (M)

2000-06-03.title: Clinton Urges United Europe to Include Russia
2000-06-03.abstract: Pres Clinton, speaking in Aachen, Germany, urges America's European allies to invite Russia into their military and political unions; says that if Russia decides against becoming a member of NATO and European Union, those two organizations must make sure that their eastward expansion does not create barriers to trade, travel and security cooperation with Russia; receives Charlemagne Award for his contribution to European unity; photo (M)

2000-06-04.title: Rebel Outpost on the Fringes of Cyberspace
2000-06-04.abstract: Small international group of computer enthusiasts plan to establish on June 5 'data haven' known as Havenco on Sealand, abandoned World War II military fortress six miles off England's coast; are hoping installation, connected to Internet by high-speed microwave and satellite links, will become refuge from governments increasingly trying to regulate and tame Internet; map; photo (M)

2000-06-04.title: Matan Has Two Mommies, and Israel Is Talking
2000-06-04.abstract: Israeli High Court orders government to register American-Israeli Nicole Berner-Kadish as second mother of four-year-old Matan Berner-Kadish, who she adopted in California, and to list him on her national identity card; boy, conceived through artificial insemination of Berner-Kadish's lesbian companion Ruti Berner-Kadish, had been planned as child of both women; decision has drawn howls of protest from country's religious right; photo (M)

2000-06-04.title: Growing Club of Left-Leaning Leaders Strains to Find a Focus
2000-06-04.abstract: Pres Clinton and 12 other left-of-center presidents and prime ministers who year ago branded themselves as leaders of new 'Third Way' meet for summit in Berlin; banish concept in favor of new mantra of 'Progressive Governance' but are unable to define its meaning; seemingly agree that they stand for new brand of social-democratic leaders who support free trade and globalization yet are determined to soften their harsher aspects through strong government support for social justice (M)

2000-06-04.title: CLINTON AND PUTIN MEET AT KREMLIN WITH WIDE AGENDA
2000-06-04.abstract: Pres Clinton arrives in Moscow in final Russian visit; meets with new Pres Vladimir V Putin in Kremlin, where both discuss full range of issues, including search for compromise on antimissile defense at three-hour working dinner; encounter is described as businesslike, congenial and easygoing; major issues facing both leaders and some planned highlights of Clinton's visit discussed; photo (M)

2000-06-04.title: Terrorism Panel Faults U.S. Effort on Iran and 1996 Bombing
2000-06-04.abstract: Report by bipartisan National Commission on Terrorism faults Clinton administration for not doing enough to press Iran for cooperation in investigation of 1996 bombing of Khobar Towers military complex in Saudi Arabia that killed 19 American servicemen, despite evidence of Iranian involvement; concludes administration has done little to mount international campaign to put diplomatic pressure on Iran to cooperate; its findings discussed (M)

2000-06-05.title: Sense of Urgency for Clinton on Arms Issue
2000-06-05.abstract: Administration officials had insisted that the meeting between the American and Russian presidents should not be viewed as focusing on arms control. But it was. The main purpose was to soften the Kremlin's resistance to American plans for a limited missile defense and to pave the way for a new arms control framework by the end of the Clinton presidency.     Two days of meetings, however, failed to produce progress on either count, and the prospects for a breakthrough on Mr. Clinton's watch seem as dim as ever.

2000-06-05.title: London Journal; Queen Breaks the Ice: Camilla's Out of the Fridge
2000-06-05.abstract: Queen Elizabeth II, in noteworthy and unexpected warming of relations, meets Camilla Parker Bowles, mistress to Prince of Wales, for first time since their affair began; encounter was at 60th birthday party for exiled King Constantine of Greece at Charles's Highgrove estate in Gloucestershire; photo (M)

2000-06-05.title: Threats by Israeli Settlers Raise Fears of Another Assassination
2000-06-05.abstract: Israel's secret service tightens security around Prime Min Ehud Barak as some Jewish settlers in Jerusalem warn domestic climate has potential to turn ugly and violent quickly if Israeli-Palestinian peace talks move forward; Barak issues warning against any attempt to use violence, or threat of violence, to derail peacemaking plans which he sees as continuation of work by late mentor, Yitzhak Rabin (M)

2000-06-05.title: China Phone Service Scraps Plan With U.S. Firm
2000-06-05.abstract: China's second telephone carrier, China Unicom, abandons plans to use current wireless telephone standards developed by Qualcomm, American company; plans to build network using later-generation version of standard, known as CDMA; says it cannot risk investing in current technology that may soon be obsolete (S)

2000-06-05.title: Americans Enjoy 70 Healthy Years, Behind Europe, U.N. Says
2000-06-05.abstract: Report by World Health Organization says US is behind almost every country in Europe, as well as Canada, Australia and Israel in what organization calls 'healthy life expectancy;' report measures life expectancy in new way by factoring in diseases and disabilities likely to rob people of lifetime of good health; says Japan leads world in healthy life expectancy, at 74.5 years; Sierra Leone is last; child born in 1999 will live healthy life of fewer than 26 years (M)

2000-06-06.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-06-06.abstract: EUROPE    NORTHERN IRELAND: FLAG DEBATE -- The Northern Ireland Assembly met for the first time since home rule was suspended by Britain in February and spent the day debating whether the Union Jack, the Irish tricolor, both flags or no flag should fly over official buildings. The Rev. Ian Paisley, head of the hard-line Ulster Democratic Party that has vowed to disrupt the power-sharing government, protested the refusal of two ministers from Sinn Fein, political ally of the Irish Republican Army, to raise the British flag at their offices.     Warren Hoge (NYT)    SPAIN: THOUSANDS PROTEST KILLING -- Thousands of people held silent vigils across the country to protest the death of Jesus Maria Pedrosa, a town councilor in the Basque city of Durango shot to death by suspected Basque rebels. Mr, Pedrosa was a member of Spain's ruling center-right Popular Party and had received threats from radical Basque separatists. The armed Basque group E.T.A. has killed five people since ending a cease-fire last December.   Benjamin Jones (NYT)

2000-06-06.title: Kunitachi City Journal; Japanese Trains Try to Shed a Gruesome Appeal
2000-06-06.abstract: Article on rising number of railway suicides in Japan, especially on line operated by East Japan Railway Company near Kunitachi City has become favorite spot; efforts of company to discourage suicides discussed; maps; photo (M)

2000-06-06.title: UKRAINE CONSENTS TO SHUT CHERNOBYL BEFORE YEAR'S END
2000-06-06.abstract: Pres Clinton leaves Russia for Ukraine on last leg of final European trip as President; addressed Russian Parliament before departure, urging legislators not to reject Washington's proposal for limited national missile defense and to make changes necessary for full participation in global trade; is welcomed in Kiev by Ukraine Pres Leonid Kuchma, who uses occasion to announce planned closing on December 15 of Chernobyl nuclear power station, site of worst radiation accident in history; says Clinton has pledged to solicit Group of Seven industrialized nations and Russia for help in shouldering costs of shutdown; Clinton pledges additional $78 million in new American aid; addessses thousands gathered in St Michael's Square; photo (M)

2000-06-06.title: Chirac Seeks Shorter Term For President
2000-06-06.abstract: French Pres Jacques Chirac proposes that presidential term be shortened from seven years to five, move that could profoundly change nature of French politics and help him in next elections; long opponent of change, he now now believes cutting term is necessary to modernize country's democracy; opinion polls indicate 75 percent of French population favors change; shorter term has been proposed by presidential candidates in last three decades, but their enthusiasm waned once they assumed office (M)

2000-06-06.title: Deprived Ukraine City Finds U.S. Help No Help
2000-06-06.abstract: Ukraine remains mired in post-Soviet purgatory of economic and social lassitude as well as endemic corruption despite US initiatives, which have been largely unsuccessful, to help nation become economically independent; many officials complain that country's agenda for economic development never seems as important as strategic objectives of US and its Western allies; economic hardships resulting from decision to give up, at US insistence, multibillion-dollar contract to construct nuclear power plant in Iran discussed; plant is being built by Russia, which is expected to earn $1 billion; map; photos (M)

2000-06-07.title: Fujimori Proposes Plans to Strengthen Democratic Safeguards
2000-06-07.abstract: Peru's Pres Alberto K Fujimori promises new efforts to strengthen democratic safeguards in reaction to flurry of international criticism over his re-election in vote widely viewed as flawed; pledges to reduce size and powers of intelligence service and to overhaul judicial system (M)

2000-06-07.title: Mexican TV, Unshackled by Reform, Fights for Viewers
2000-06-07.abstract: Race for ratings between Televisa and TV Azteca, Mexico's major television networks, is defining coverage of presidential election campaign as never before; government reforms since 1994 has opened new freedoms for press, and competition for viewers has intensified with fury (M)

2000-06-07.title: G.O.P. Senator Frees Millions for U.N. Mission in Sierra Leone
2000-06-07.abstract: Republican Sen Judd Gregg, chairman of Senate appropriations subcimmittee who has blocked hundreds of millions of dollars approved by Congress for UN peacekeeping missions, agrees to release $50 million for peacekeepers in Sierra Leone; claims to have forced shift in American foreign policy (M)

2000-06-07.title: Kars Journal; As War Wanes, Tourists Return to Turkey's East
2000-06-07.abstract: Tourists begin returning to mostly Kurdish eastern region of Turkey, less than year after peace returned to area and their arrival suggests tourism industry could become source of income and stability in economically depressed area; are flocking to Kars and spectacular ruined city of Ani, which was thriving Armenian capital 10 centuries ago; maps; photo (M)

2000-06-07.title: Britain's Green Prince and His Family Differ on Altered Crops
2000-06-07.abstract: Britain's Princess Anne and father Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh, voice sharp public disagreements with Prince Charles over genetically modified crops; Princess Anne, in magazine interview, dismisses as oversimplification idea that all farming should be organic and Prince Phillip says introduction of exotic species into Britain's environment has been far more harmful; Prince Charles only allows organic farming on his Highgrove estate and speaks out frequently on perils of genetic engineering (M)

2000-06-08.title: Albright Tells Syrians an Accord for Palestinians Is First Goal
2000-06-08.abstract: State Sec Madeleine K Albright, between separate meetings with Egyptian Pres Hosni Mubarak and Syrian Min Farouk al-Shara, Cairo, says Clinton administration is willing to push again for Israeli-Syrian peace talks, but that its main goal is settlement between Israel and Palestinians; says United States sticks to own timetable for Palestinian pact, despite Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak's coalition problems; Israel pulls back from two small outposts north of provisional border with Lebanon newly demarcated by United Nations; UN cites further minor Israeli violation of withdrawal requirements (M)

2000-06-08.title: Egyptian At Center On Rights Of Women
2000-06-08.abstract: Interview with Suzanne Mubarak, wife of Pres Hosni Mubarak, who leads Egyptian delegation to United Nations conference on implementing rights for women drafted at 1995 Beijing conference; she says women must speak against false notions of Islam promoted by extremists who would deny women education and jobs; photo (M)

2000-06-08.title: Turmoil in Israel
2000-06-08.abstract: Prime Minister Ehud Barak's government was thrown into tumult when Parliament approved a preliminary bill to disperse and head toward new elections. The bill requires three more hearings to become law. But it could break up the ruling coalition and create a chaotic interlude at a diplomatically inopportune moment.  Article, Page A8.

2000-06-08.title: Government of Israel Thrown Into Tumult by Vote in Parliament to Call New Elections
2000-06-08.abstract: Israeli Parliament, 61-48, approves preliminary bill to disperse and head toward new elections, throwing Prime Min Ehud Barak's government into turmoil; three of six parties in Barak's broad coalition vote for bill, including ultra-Orthodox Shas Party; bill requires three more hearings to become law; vote, even if it fails to spur new elections, could break up Barak's coalition and distract Barak during final peace talks with Palestinians; Barak addresses nation; urges Israelis to trust him to restore calm; photos (M)

2000-06-08.title: Turkey Considers Scaling Back Military Challenge to Greece
2000-06-08.abstract: Turkish Prime Min Bulent Ecevit weighs proposal by adviser, retired Adm Guven Erkaya, to disband 100,000-man Aegean Army, which serves in zone of military confrontation with Greece; prominence given plan, which general staff says is not on agenda, has spurred national debate over issue; debate follows nearly year of improving ties with Greece; Greece and Turkey are locked in tangle of sovereignty disputes over sea lanes, air rights and barren islets in Aegean Sea (M)

2000-06-09.title: A Pattern of Unsolved Greek Terrorism Cases
2000-06-09.abstract: Killing of British diplomat Brig Stephen Saunders in Athens comes in midst of growing debate in Washington over Greece's record in curbing anti-Western terrorism within its borders; only a few days ago, bipartisan US commission on terrorism recomended that Clinton administration consider imposing sanctions on Greece for failing to fully cooperate with United States in combatting terrorism; failure of Greek authorities to solve anti-American terrorism cases over 25-year period has turned group called November 17 into one of most mysterious and most successful terrorist groups in world; not even names of group's leaders are known (M)

2000-06-09.title: Reporter's Notebook; Life Is Now a Fast-Forward Blur For America's Most Frequent Flier
2000-06-09.abstract: Reporter's Notebook article on Pres Clinton's 'jet-lag diplomacy' in squeezing into his heavy schedule a 13-hour trip to Japan to spend a few hours attending memorial service for late Prime Min Keizo Obuchi; photo (M)

2000-06-09.title: Brazil Opens Files on Region's Abuses in Age of Dictators
2000-06-09.abstract: Military dictatorships of South America during 1970's organized secret program, known as Operation Condor, to kidnap and kill one another's opponents; now the democratic governments that succeeded them are joining together, albeit tentatively and somewhat reluctantly, in effort to determine true extent and toll of that collaboration; Brazil's high court recently ordered that all documents relating to Operation Condor and to case of three Argentinians who disappeared in Brazil when both countries were under military rule be released to Argentinian authorities; rights group say that government of Brazilian Pres Fernando Henrique Cardoso is being too slow in producing documents (M)

2000-06-09.title: Britain Tries To Reassure Sierra Leone Before Pullout
2000-06-09.abstract: British Foreign Sec Robin Cook tries to reassure anxious Sierra Leoneans of Britain's long-term commitment to its former West African colony, even as British troops prepare to withdraw and leave country's security in hands of United Nations peacekeeping force; on one-day trip to capital Freetown, Cook meets with Pres Ahmed Tejan Kabbah, leaders of fragile pro-government coalition, and military heads of a UN force that had teetered on edge of collapse in early May before British arrived; photo (M)

2000-06-09.title: British Attache Is Assassinated On Greek Street
2000-06-09.abstract: Brig Stephen Saunders, senior military attache at British Embassy in Athens, is gunned down by two gunmen identified as members of left-wing terrorist group November 17; Saunders is first British official assassinated in an attack linked to November 17, but killing is 23rd attributed to group, which emerged in 1975 with slaying of Richard Welch, Central Intelliegence Agency station chief in Athens, and three other United States officials; in last 25 years, no member of November 17 has been arrested; State Department report in April and special US commission report only a few days ago both criticized Greece for being disturbingly passive in response to terrorist activities; photos (M)

2000-06-10.title: Oxford-Cambridge Rivalry Is Complicated by Harvard
2000-06-10.abstract: Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation is considering a request from Cambridge University for a $200 million scholarship endowment to bring 230 foreign graduate students to Cambridge each year; meanwhile, controversy over Britain's elite universities is rekindled after Laura Spence, a high school student from a public state-run school, applies to study medicine at Oxford, is rejected after an interview, despite high grades, and goes on to receive a $100,000 scholarship to Harvard University (M)

2000-06-10.title: World Briefing
2000-06-10.abstract: AFRICA    ETHIOPIA, ERITREA: POSSIBLE PACT  Eritrea said it had accepted cease-fire proposals from the Organization of African Unity to end its two-year border war with Ethiopia, which is expected to respond today. News of the proposals, after negotiations in Algiers, coincided with charges by Eritrea that Ethiopia had unleashed an all-out attack along the front close to Assab.    (Reuters)    NIGERIA: OIL PRICE STRIKE  A general strike over sharp fuel price increases halted all flights at Nigerian airports, and the action also spread to seaports and the oil industry, officials said. The commercial capital, Lagos, was paralyzed for the second day in what threatens to be the biggest disruption in Africa's biggest oil exporter since military rule ended a year ago. Unions rejected President Olusegun Obasanjo's offer to halve the 50 percent rise, instituted as part of a policy of ending government subsidies.    (Reuters)

2000-06-10.title: Famine in North Korea Creates Steady Human Flow Into China
2000-06-10.abstract: Estimated 100,000 to 200,000 North Koreans are living illegally in China as result of their government's failure to solve its longstanding food crisis; many fear arrest and deportation to a North Korean jail; say the food aid arriving in North Korea from abroad is being sold on black market at prices that are beyond their reach; aid workers say government seems to have given up on providing food for the whole country and is forcing remote regions to fend for themselves; up to two million people have died from food shortages over last five years; deaths rates are dropping, but that is largely because of Western food aid and because North Koreans have developed survival strategies to blunt what United Nations experts have called 'slow-motion famine'; photo; map (M)

2000-06-10.title: Barak Facing Old Dilemma
2000-06-10.abstract: Deborah Sontag analysis of the diminished options availabe to Prime Min Ehud Barak after Israeli Parliament vote to disperse and go into new elections; photo (M)

2000-06-10.title: Tsakane Journal; Apartheid Still Burdens A Girl Who Didn't Fit
2000-06-10.abstract: South Africans are reminded of the pain that apartheid caused ordinary people by a television documentary on Sandra Laing, now 44, who grew up a black child in a white family; she was expelled from her white school and classified as black in 1966; her parents had her reclassified as white, but then disowned her when they caught her dating a black man; Laing recently visted her mother for the first time since then in her nursing home; her brothers refuse to see her; photos; map (M)

2000-06-11.title: Putin Seeks Allies in Quest To Fight U.S. Missile Plan
2000-06-11.abstract: Russian Pres Vladimir V Putin seeks allies in his campaign against US plans for national missile defense system; recently visited Brussels and Rome and plans to visit North Korea; photo (M)

2000-06-11.title: A New Hurdle to Peace
2000-06-11.abstract: Elaine Sciolino analysis holds that for Clinton administration, death of Pres Hafez al-Assad makes possibility of brokering peace between Israelis and Syrians even more remote and desire to help forge peace agreement between Israelis and Palestinians even more intense (M)

2000-06-11.title: Presidential Challenger in Mexico Pitches Tent in Two Camps
2000-06-11.abstract: Vicente Fox is mounting most serious challenge to Mexico's governing party in presidential election in 70 years; he has attracted supporters from across political spectrum; Francisco Labastida of governing Institutional Revolutionary Party is his main rival in July 2 presidential race; leftist candidate Cuauhtemoc Cardenas is running distant third; Fox photo (M)

2000-06-11.title: Apprehension and Hope For Stability in Mideast
2000-06-11.abstract: Death of Pres Hafez al-Assad, who managed to keep Syria a political power in Middle East, brings expressions of apprehension and cautious hope for stability from regional and world leaders; photo (M)

2000-06-11.title: Syria's Leader Assad Dies, Clouding Mideast Prospects
2000-06-11.abstract: Pres Hafez al-Assad, standard-bearer of Arab nationalism and anti-Zionist fervor who ruled Syria for three decades, dies at age 69; his death from heart attack casts cloud of uncertainty over dynamics of peace and conflict in Middle East; his son, Bashar, is expected to succeed him; photos (M)

2000-06-12.title: Addis Ababa Journal; Where Stuffed Hyena Dwells With Emperor's Bed
2000-06-12.abstract: Sometime when Sebatu Gebre Yesus was  young  he cannot remember when -- he began collecting things. This is unusual for at least two reasons. Not many Ethiopians are collectors. At 79, far along a life of quiet closeness to power and heartbreak because of it, he claims to be the only one.     Second, he is a collector who makes no distinction between the priceless and the junky. A pair of Baccarat chandeliers from the palace of Emperor Haile Selassie sit not far from a souvenir thermometer larded with a fat Napoleon. Bronzes from Benin, worth a small fortune, are just down the way from a bongo with a split drumhead. He owns a Monet, along with bizarre portraits of unknown children. He will take orphaned runt or pedigree, and love it.

2000-06-12.title: Petar Mladenov, Bulgarian Communist Leader, 63
2000-06-12.abstract: Petar T. Mladenov, who served for 18 years as foreign minister of Bulgaria under the Communist strongman Todor Zhivkov, then helped to oust him and became the country's president, died on May 31 in Sofia, the state news agency B.T.A. reported. He was 63.    Mr. Mladenov underwent bypass surgery in Houston in 1986 and was believed to have been in frail health since then.

2000-06-12.title: Maverick Chinese Province Fears Opening of Trade
2000-06-12.abstract: Every Monday for seven years, Wang Tai-ching has boarded a ferry in Hong Kong and chugged up the muddy waters of the Pearl River delta. His destination is a factory in Zhongshan, one of the slapdash cities that have sprouted in Guangdong Province over the last decade, crowding out rice paddies and duck ponds with smokestacks and elevated highways.    Mr. Wang, and thousands of Hong Kong entrepreneurs like him, have helped transform Guangdong into the fastest-growing region in China -- a capitalist hothouse on the South China Sea. As China moves closer to its dream of entering the World Trade Organization, however, people here are nervous.

2000-06-12.title: Lord MacLehose, 82, Governor Of Hong Kong and China Scholar
2000-06-12.abstract: Lord MacLehose, a British diplomat and China scholar who was London's longest-serving governor of Hong Kong, from 1971 to 1982, died on May 27 in Ayrshire, Scotland. He was 82 and had run a sheep farm at Beoch outside Maybole since his retirement.    His term as governor of Hong Kong spanned its transition from a backwater trading post to a fledgling economic powerhouse. The colony reverted to Chinese sovereignty in 1997, an event he had anticipated by working to clarify the ambiguous and often testy relationship between Britain and mainland China.

2000-06-12.title: Village Loyal to Assad and Syria Fears Partition by U.N. Mapmakers
2000-06-12.abstract: Carrying black flags and a flower-wreathed portrait of Hafez al-Assad, the people of this border village held a memorial ceremony today for the leader of the country they call their own.    The villagers, members of Mr. Assad's minority Alawite sect, chanted and wept in a demonstration of loyalty to Syria that was repeated today in the neighboring Druse villages of the Golan Heights, which was captured by Israel from Syria in the 1967 Arab-Israeli war.

2000-06-13.title: After High-Pressure Years, Contractors Tone Down Missile Defense Lobbying
2000-06-13.abstract: Nation's military contractors have toned down lobbying for $60 billion in potential business from proposed antimissile defense system; feel they have already done work, giving over last decade $49 million in campaign contributions to Washington politicians and additional $2 million in more subtle and indirect campaign they say has helped create atmosphere in which pressure to build antimissile system weighs heavily on both parties; chart (M)

2000-06-13.title: Rajesh Pilot, 55, Keen Backer Of Indian Congress Party, Dies
2000-06-13.abstract: Rajesh Pilot, longtime member of Indian Parliament and an independent-minded politician with strong following among peasants of northern India, dies at age 55; photo (M)

2000-06-13.title: Congo Is Increasing Resistance To Peace Efforts, U.N. Says
2000-06-13.abstract: Hundreds of rock-throwing demonstrators have attacked UN offices in Congo in recent days, development agency officials say seem to reflect government-inspired campaign in which Pres Laurent Kabila is hardening his resistance to internationally supported political talks about country's future; orchestrated attacks in Kinshasa and Kisangani call into question not only immediate hopes for high-level meeting on Congo politics in UN Security Council, but also future of UN peacekeeping force in country (M)

2000-06-13.title: Exiled Relative Issues Challenge to Syria's Heir to Power
2000-06-13.abstract: Syria prepares for state funeral for late Pres Hafez amid reports that exiled brother, Rifat al-Assad, has proclaimed himself rightful heir to power and will challenge appointment of Assad's son, Bashar al-Assad; Rifaat al-Assad has lived in Europe since unsuccessful attempt to oust brother in 1983; has maintained platform for activities through satellite television, Arab News Network, operated by one of sons; photo (M)

2000-06-13.title: Seoul Leader Lands in North for Meeting
2000-06-13.abstract: South Korean Pres Kim Dae Jung, arrives in North Korean capital of Pyongyang for first-ever series of talks between leaders of both countries almost exactly 50 years since start of Korean War; receives warm welcome from Pres Kim Jong Il and throng of thousands on arrival; South Koreans harbor sense of hope about visit, but also deep uncertainties about dealing with North, one of world most secretive and unpredictable nations; efforts at rapprochement between both nations over past two years described; map; photos (M)

2000-06-14.title: Tax Havens Face OECD Threat of Sanctions
2000-06-14.abstract: BRUSSELS—  The noose is tightening on financial centers that permit money laundering, tax evasion and bribery.

2000-06-14.title: Chile Military To Search For Victims Of Its Rule
2000-06-14.abstract: Chilean military reaches accord with moderate human rights lawyers to search for remains of 1,200 dissidents who disappeared, 1973-90, during Gen Augusto Pinochet's dictatorship; accord is not likely to lead to new prosecutions of human rights offenders, but is first time that military has admitted role in disappearances (M)

2000-06-14.title: U.S. Ambassador to U.N. Calls for Changes in Peacekeeping
2000-06-14.abstract: Richard C Holbrooke, in interview reflecting on his first year as United States ambassador to United Nations, holds UN must transform its civilian-run peacekeeping department into larger and more effective military-style operation to avoid repeated humiliations in riskier missions it is undertaking; cites need for military professionals, in New York and in field, to bolster peacekeeping efforts; photo (M)

2000-06-14.title: Albright Finds Syria's New Leader Willing to Pursue Talks
2000-06-14.abstract: State Sec Madeleine K Albright meets briefly with Bashar al-Assad, Syria's new leader, Damascus; Bashar says he is ready to pursue peace talks with Israel; Clinton administration has tried to broker such talks (M)

2000-06-14.title: U.S. Fines Lockheed $13 Million in China Satellite Case
2000-06-14.abstract: Lockheed Martin Corp agrees to pay $13 million fine, largest civil penalty ever under Arms Export Control Act, to settle case involving 1994 sale of satellite technology to China; accord does not call for suspension of company's right to export satellite technology; settlement may put pressure on Loral Space & Communications Corp to settle separate case (M)

2000-06-15.title: Will Turk Tell Secrets On Shooting Of the Pope?
2000-06-15.abstract: Mehmet Ali Agca, who shot Pope John Paul II in 1981, arrives in Turkey, carrying some of Turkey's darkest secrets with him; Agca, in 1970's, was part of network who believed that it was their mission to cleanse Turkey of leftist influence; considerable evidence has emerged to suggest that network was closely tied to far-right politicians, police commanders and intelliegence officers; Agca was also convicted in absentia of 1979 murder of liberal newpaper editor Abdi Ipekci after he escaped from prison while awaiting trial; Agca has served 19 years of life term in an Italian prison, but was released for extradition to Turkey at urging of the Pope, who had visited and forgiven him (M)

2000-06-15.title: Russia Says Remains Are Those Of Envoy Abducted in Chechnya
2000-06-15.abstract: Russian government says it has identified remains found in Chechnya this spring as those of Maj Gen Gennadi Shpigun, federal envoy whose kidnapping helped touch off Russia's latest war against region's secessionist rebels (M)

2000-06-15.title: Warm Welcome for 'Putin the German'
2000-06-15.abstract: Russian Pres Vladimir V Putin arrives in Germany for first visit since taking power; he is greeted by newspaper headlines like 'Putin the German' that suggest German hopes vested in a Russian president who formerly resided in East Germany; Putin's visit is seen as part of ambitious strategy to hitch Russia firmly to Western Europe, prod Germany to look east as much as west, and, if possible, drive wedge between Europe and United States; Germany's susceptibility to these overtures is certainly limited, given country's fierce attachment to NATO, but Chancellor Gerhard Schroder's government has left little doubt of its determination to strengthen Russian relations; photo (M)

2000-06-15.title: CLINTON LAWYERS GIVE A GO-AHEAD TO MISSILE SHIELD
2000-06-15.abstract: Administration lawyers advise Pres Clinton that he could begin building first phase of national missile defense system without violating 1972 arms control treaty with Russia; lawyers' interpretations, which were drafted at request of White House, are likely to be rejected by Russia, and Clinton has not made a decision on them, but they offer Clinton a way to announce that United States will go ahead with missile defenses while letting next administration decide whether to break Antiballistic Missilie Treaty; prospect of withdrawing from treaty has already threatened to undermine relations with Russia, as well as with European allies, who view treaty as a foundation of nuclear arms control (M)

2000-06-15.title: 120 Charged, With Promise of More to Come : U.S. Busts Stock Scam With Ties to the Mob
2000-06-15.abstract: NEW YORK—  With legal filings that read like notes for a crime novel, federal officials on Wednesday announced what they described as the largest stock-fraud crackdown in U.S. history in a case that included all five of New York's alleged Cosa Nostra families as well as a member of the city's police department.

2000-06-16.title: Koreas Plan for Olympics
2000-06-16.abstract: New agreement between North and South Korea pushes for cooperation on athletic level as well as political, and both sides are speaking encouragingly about possibility of marching together at opening ceremonies of 2000 Summer Olympics in Sydney, Australia (M)

2000-06-16.title: Kandahar Journal; Every Step Is a Risk in Booby-Trapped Backyards
2000-06-16.abstract: Afghanistan, one of world's most enduring battlegrounds, is also among its most booby-trapped landscapes, with estimated five to seven million land mines buried in its soil; but 10-year mine-clearing operation under United Nations auspices is making significant progress; in Kandahar, country's second-largest city, housing is so scarce that families have taken shelter surrounded by rubble of what is called Minefield 179, willing to risk explosives buried in their new backyard even as de-miners work around them; map; photos (Kandahar Journal) (M)

2000-06-16.title: Serb Opposition Leader Is Shot in Montenegro
2000-06-16.abstract: Well-known Serbian opposition leader Vuk Draskovic is shot and wounded by gunmen in Montenegro, where he fled last month from Serbia and sought protection from Pres Milo Djukanovic; Draskovic is leading campaign for early elections to remove Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic from power (M)

2000-06-16.title: World Briefing
2000-06-16.abstract: THE AMERICAS    CANADA: GUN LAW UPHELD  The Supreme Court has unanimously upheld the nation's gun control law, which is stricter than United States gun laws. Rejecting a challenge by 2 of Canada's territories and 6 of its 10 provinces, the court upheld the 1995 Firearms Act, which requires the licensing of all of Canada's gun owners by the end of this year and the registration of all guns by the end of 2002. Firearms owners had largely boycotted the licensing process, awaiting the outcome of the court challenge.    James Brooke (NYT)

2000-06-16.title: U.N. Opens Talks Seeking Withdrawal of Congo Combatants
2000-06-16.abstract: United Nations Security Council urges visiting ministers from a half-dozen African nations embroiled in war in Congo to join in seeking way out of desperate conflict that continues to devastate biggest country in central Africa; draft resolution expressing 'deep concern' over continuing hostilities calls for orderly withdrawal of all foreign forces; session is attend by senior officals from Congo, Angola, Rwanda, Uganda, Zambia and Zimbabwe, as well as two Congolese rebel movements and Orgaization for African Unity (M)

2000-06-17.title: Mexico's Influential Long Shot
2000-06-17.abstract: Cuauhtemoc Cardenas, battle-horse leader of Mexico's lefist opposition, runs determined campaign for president; photo; map; surge makes him pivotal player in most contested race in Mexican history, because his gain mostly takes votes away from Vicente Fox Quesada, who is also trying to oust long-ruling PRI (M)

2000-06-17.title: World Briefing
2000-06-17.abstract: THE AMERICAS    HAITI: VOTE PROTESTS -- Supporters of former President Jean-Bertrand Aristide blocked downtown Port-au-Prince, burning tires and stoning cars to demand results from local and legislative elections held May 21. Preliminary results showed Mr. Aristide's Lavalas Family party winning 16 of 17 Senate seats and more than 20 seats in the 83-member House of Deputies. An official said final results had been given to the electoral council, but no reason has been given for the delay in their publication.   (AP)

2000-06-17.title: David Heaps, 84, Human Rights Advocate
2000-06-17.abstract: David Heaps, human rights advocate, dies at 84 (S)

2000-06-17.title: Pope's Assailant, in Turkish Court, Denies Killing Editor in 1979
2000-06-17.abstract: Mehmet Ali Agca, who spent 19 years in Italian prison for shooting Pope John Paul II, returns to Turkey and denies in court that he killed Turkish newspaper editor, Abdi Ipekci, in 1979; he escaped after 1979 arrest and was tried in absentia and sentenced to death (S)

2000-06-17.title: After Korean Talks, Albright Plans Seoul and Beijing Trip
2000-06-17.abstract: Sec Madeline Albright will visit South Korea and China to capitalize on momentum of Korean summit meeting (S)

2000-06-18.title: Dispute Over Jewish Graves Draws Protesters to Prague
2000-06-18.abstract: International Orthodox Jewish organizations renew their protests against planned construction of administrative building over remnants of 13th century Jewish cemetery in downtown Prague; fear is that construction would disturb medieval graves; Czech police have arrested at least dozen Orthodox Jewish rabbis from United States, England and Belgium who illegally entered construction site; rabbis insist they found destroyed graves and bone fragments on ground, charge denied by representatives of insurance company that owns site; dispute has opened rift between leaders of Prague's small Jewish community and foreign rabbis (M)

2000-06-18.title: National Guard Unit Adds Dimension to a Peacekeeper Role
2000-06-18.abstract: Changing of the Guard, article in series about 49th Armored Division of Texas National Guard, deployed in Bosnia as part of NATO peacekeeping force; nation's citizen-soldiers are bringing to the job something their active-duty counterparts cannot: experience and skills honed in civilian world; many would argue that Guard and Reserve are in fact better prepared for subtle tasks of rebuilding nation devastated by war; say they are better at dealing with civilians; men and women of 49th were computer programmers, teachers, lawyers and laborers until they were mobilized in February; their skills have already paid dividends in Bosnia; photos; graph (L)

2000-06-18.title: Assad's Son Gets Syrian Party's Blessing
2000-06-18.abstract: Syria's Baath Party, support beneath 30-year rule of Hafez al-Assad, gathers to pledge allegiance to his son, Bashar, and to vest him formally with political authority he has quietly exercised in past two years of his father's illness; party delegates indicate their readiness to install younger Assad, an ophthalmologist by training, as member of their national governing board and to endorse him for job of president; Bashar Assad has expressed interest in bringing Syria into modern information age; he may be limited in his ability to impose modernization on hidebound one-party system that has run nation's economy and social life for its own benefit; photo (M)

2000-06-18.title: Lone Voice No Longer, a Japanese Gadfly Catches On
2000-06-18.abstract: Zensaku Sakurai, sidewalk crusader who berates Japan's leaders for what he calls their arrogance and corruption, is attracting more listeners in country where direct criticism of others strikes uncomfortable chord; Sakurai's movement is most successful of several grass-roots movements that are catching fire; members of governing party and its coalition partners, already on defensive as hotly contested national elections draw near, call such movements undemocratic and appeal to national media not to report about them; Sakurai has long railed against government; how his movement has gained steam, finally, is illustration of unpredictable nature of political cross-fertilization brought about by technology, particularly Internet (M)

2000-06-18.title: TV's Impious Puppets: On Kremlin's Hit List?
2000-06-18.abstract: Litmus test of Kremlin's intentions regarding future of free speech in Russia may be whether it tries to crack down on television satire called Kukly, which features puppets that are dead-on renditions of balding Communists, woolly-headed right-wingers, as well as Pres Vladimir V Putin and his staff chief, Aleksandr Voloshin; Kukly, most popular television show in country, is by turns vicious, obscene, hilarious and knowing; there is growing sense among program's staff that program is on collision course with Kremlin; photo (M)

2000-06-19.title: New Pragmatism in South Africa's Foreign Policy Disappoints Some Old Supporters
2000-06-19.abstract: South African Pres Thabo Mbeki, year after succeeding Nelson Mandela, is increasingly embracing pragmatic foreign policy seemingly driven as much by national interest as by humanitarian principle; relations with Zimbabwe cited; as Zimbabwe lurches toward parliamentary elections and growing number of opposition party supporters are killed, beaten and intimidated, Mbeki government has been strangely silent; has failed to condemn Pres Robert Mugabe who is widely blamed for instigating and condoning worst spate of political violence in decade; Mbeki has praised Mugabe in public while prodding him in private--treading carefully with significant trading partner and ally and ignoring protests from domestic and international human rights activists; analysts expect to see more behind-the-scenes negotiating and and less tongue-lashing from continent's biggest economic and military force; map; photo (M)

2000-06-19.title: Portals Struggle to Turn Virtual Shoppers Into Real Buyers
2000-06-19.abstract: SUPPOSE you manage a mall where visitors merely window-shop and rarely enter stores to make purchases. If your merchants can't survive, you lose, too. That is the phenomenon plaguing many Web retailers and portals today.

2000-06-19.title: Eastern Europe First Needs to Build Up Business : The Next Step:Charting E-Commerce's Future
2000-06-19.abstract: NEW YORK—  Some years ago, a software company in Russia called Trio-Plus did a great business selling its products through mail order to the remote regions outside Moscow and St. Petersburg.

2000-06-19.title: An Obstacle On Lebanon Is Overcome In the U.N.
2000-06-19.abstract: UN Security Council, after 16 hours of deadlock, gives qualified endorsement of Sec Gen Kofi Annan's certification that Israel has withdrawn from southern Lebanon while expressing concern over Beirut's charges that Israelis continue to encroach on Lebanese soil; endorsement opens way to stationing of UN peacekeepers across southern Lebanon and authorizing them to help government restore authority in often chaotic border region; threat by Annan to scrap imminent visit to Lebanon if council declined to support his confirmation of withdrawal noted; photo (M)

2000-06-19.title: Dangerous Coal Mines Take Human Toll in China
2000-06-19.abstract: Chinese officials admit country, which produces more coal than any other, also has probably world's worst mine safety record; coal bureau official says deaths hover around 10,000 annually, but mining experts say figure represents undercount and does not begin to account for nonfatal injuries; international data indicate for every million tons of coal produced, China has 11 times as many reported fatalities as Russia, 15 times as many as India and 182 times as many as US; mining conditions in some regions, such as in Datong in Shanxi Province, described; map; chart; photo (M)

2000-06-20.title: The Dutch Seek to Legalize Long-Tolerated Euthanasia
2000-06-20.abstract: Netherlands, where mercy killing and assisted suicide of terminally ill patients have been widely tolerated, will introduce legislation to decriminalize such acts if certain criteria are met, giving country least restrictive laws on mercy killing and assisted suicide in world; aim of new legislation is to make sure that what happens during patient's final hours happens in open, where it can be scrutinized and regulated; doctors' actions in helping patients die would no longer be routinely reviewed by prosecutors, but by committee of doctors, lawyers and ethicists; patient's request to die must be 'durable;' he or she must face unbearable suffering and have no reasonable alternative to death; doctor must also consult with at least one other independent physician before helping patient to die; movement toward legalization of mercy killing and assisted suicide elsewhere in Europe noted; chart; photo (M)

2000-06-20.title: U.S. and China Agree on Steps To Fight Drugs
2000-06-20.abstract: US and China agree to step up cooperation in fight against illegal drugs, especially heroin and methamphetamine that have emerged as scourges in both countries and throughout much of Asia; sign first formal agreement to share information and evidence related to drug smuggling; Gen Barry R McCaffrey, director of White House drug-control office hails agreement as 'important moment' (S)

2000-06-20.title: Bodies of 58 Asians in Dover: An 'Evil Trade in People'
2000-06-20.abstract: British customs officers searching consignment of imported tomatoes aboard Dutch truck in Channel port of Dover discover bodies of 58 Asian men and women inside who apparently died of suffocation; two survivors are taken to hospitals and driver of truck is arrested; Prime Min Tony Blair says discovery underscores importance of stamping out 'evil trade' in people; Home Sec Jack Straw says incident will be subject of major police investigation; at least 20 criminal groups are reportedly involved in bringing illegal immigrants into Britain; small groups of bewildered, frightened and sickly foreigners emerging from backs of vehicles have become common sight at Dover and other Channel ports; map; photo (M)

2000-06-20.title: Mexican President Denounces Predictions of Election Fraud
2000-06-20.abstract: Mexico's Pres Ernesto Zedillo assails opposition candidates, who warned of possible irregularities in July 2 presidential election; accuses them of sowing mistrust in federal agency overseeing vote and of trying to discredit election with 'intransigent' positions; comments seem aimed at right-of-center candidate Vicente Fox Quesada and left-leaning contender Cuauhtemoc Cardenas Solorzano; both have been battling each other on campaign trail, but recently joined pact to guard against voter coercion before election and against fraud during balloting (M)

2000-06-20.title: Greece Gains Entry Into Single-Currency Zone
2000-06-20.abstract: SANTA MARIA DA FEIRA, Portugal—  In what was called a step toward modernity, Greece formally won acceptance as the 12th member of the European Monetary Union on Monday, and the Greek prime minister, Costas Simitis, said the move would quickly result in lower interest rates, long-term price stability and faster economic growth.

2000-06-21.title: For Zidane, Dash of Joy in His Genius
2000-06-21.abstract: AMSTERDAM—  Back in Marseille, his face still eyes the Mediterranean from a mural painting big enough for a Gadhafi or a Brezhnev. Kids come with marker pens and scribble their name on his shoulders, and locals tell, weirdly, of just-married couples posing for photos in their wedding outfits, seemingly blessed by Zizou's forever-true gaze.

2000-06-21.title: World Briefing
2000-06-21.abstract: EUROPE    NORTHERN IRELAND: BOMB THREAT -- British Army experts defused a small bomb in a bag that had been thrown over the fortified high wall surrounding the grounds of Hillsborough Castle, the official home of the Northern Ireland secretary, Peter Mandelson, outside Belfast. Mr. Mandelson was in London at the time. A Downing Street spokesman said, ''It was found well away from the house, it was a small device and I would not want to oxygenate the story by taking up what is a comparatively minor incident.''   Warren Hoge (NYT)

2000-06-21.title: Live on Moscow TV: Quiz a Tycoon About His Arrest
2000-06-21.abstract: Vladimir Gusinsky, owner of NTV Network, Russia's largest independent media conglomerate, says he was recently arrested and jailed for three days at behest of Pres Vladimir V Putin; was accused of embezzling state property; photo (M)

2000-06-21.title: Clinton Seeks to Avoid Acting on Missile Defense System
2000-06-21.abstract: Officials say Clinton administration is quietly maneuvering to sidestep contentious decision over whether to build national missile defense system, playing down significance of Pentagon's next test of system and leaving final decision to next administration; hoping to avert diplomatic and political crisis over breaking of Antiballistic Missile Treaty of 1972, administration and military officials have begun emphasizing that Clinton faces decision in Fall 2000 only on whether defense shield is feasible to counter missile threats (M)

2000-06-21.title: Hitler Apologist Wins German Honor, and a Storm Breaks Out
2000-06-21.abstract: Award of Konrad Adenauer Prize, one of Germany's most prestigious literary prizes, to historian Ernst Nolte ignites fierce debate in Germany at time of conservative and reactionary intellectual ferment in Europe, and divides country's leading historical institutes; Nolte has argued Hitler's anti-Semitism had rational core and that Nazism was in essence riposte to Bolshevism; photo (M)

2000-06-22.title: Mexico Lets U.S. Election Observer Return
2000-06-22.abstract: Mexico lets Ted Lewis, American who heads large delegation of Global Exchange human rights group election observers and was expelled from Mexico in April, return and monitor July presidential vote (M)

2000-06-22.title: A Secretive Team, on Guard for Nuclear Terror
2000-06-22.abstract: WASHINGTON—  - The Nuclear Emergency Search Team — the Energy Department unit likened to a nuclear bomb squad that kept sensitive information on the two computer disks that disappeared at Los Alamos — is a highly secretive outfit little-known to the public.

2000-06-22.title: Rome Journal; Youth Had Its Day: Revenge of the Gerontocracy
2000-06-22.abstract: Former Prime Min Giulio Andreotti, 81, featured in advertisements for Web site, symbolizes revenge of Italy's gerontocracy; older leaders are making comeback despite emergence of new generation of business and political leaders in wake of political corruption probe that toppled old political order; Prime Min Giuliano Amato, for example, held cabinet posts in many previous governments; photo (M)

2000-06-22.title: Congo Leader Shuts Down Africa Mediation Effort
2000-06-22.abstract: Pres Laurent Kabila says he shuts down Organization of African Unity effort to open talks among warring factions in Congo because group's mediator, Sir Ketumile Masire openly criticized government; says Masire's Kinshasa offices were sealed and his staff dispersed; is hopeful he can work out deal with Rwandan Pres Paul Kagame to end war; charges Ugandan troops seek only to loot Congo; State Department urges Kabila to end what it calls violation of 1999 peace accord; photo (M)

2000-06-22.title: North Korea's Missile Pledge Paves the Way for New Talks
2000-06-22.abstract: Clinton administration says North Korea has reaffirmed its pledge to stop flight-testing its long-range missile, paving way for talks on further curbs on missile program; says pledge does not alter Clinton's resolve to move ahead with missile defense program that has largely been justified by North Korean threat; State Sec Madeleine K Albright arrives in China for talks on range of issues (M)

2000-06-23.title: Britain Charges Dutch Truck Driver in Migrants' Deaths
2000-06-23.abstract: Perry Wacker, Dutch driver of a truck that became the sealed tomb of 58 illegal Chinese immigrants packed into its airtight container, is charged with manslaughter in Britain, as well as with trying to bring victims into the country and with facilitating the entry of the two survivors; none of victims have been identified, and Tan Wah-piow, London-based lawyer, calls on authorities to grant amnesty to any of victims relatives who may be in Britain illegally so that they can come forward and claim their dead; photo (M)

2000-06-23.title: 15 Countries Named as Potential Money-Laundering Havens
2000-06-23.abstract: Financial Action Task Force, body created by Group of 7 wealthy countries to fight money laundering, names 15 countries and territories as potential havens for ill-gotten wealth; they are Israel, Philippines, Russia, Bahamas, Cayman Islands, Cook Islands, Dominica, Lebanon, Liechtenstein, Marshall Islands, Nauru, Niue, Panama, St Kitts and Nevis and St Vincent and the Grenadines (M)

2000-06-23.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-06-23.abstract: EUROPE    FRANCE: DE GAULLE RUNNING  The head of the far-right National Front, Jean-Marie Le Pen, announced that the grandson of Charles de Gaulle will head a list of candidates vying to represent the 20 districts of Paris in municipal elections next year. Mr. Le Pen said he was proud to be able to count on such an ''emblematic figure'' as the grandson, above, who is also named Charles de Gaulle. Other family members have been outraged at Mr. de Gaulle's affiliation with the National Front, a party long accused of fostering racism and anti-Semitism. Mr. de Gaulle, a lawyer, represents the party in the European Parliament.    Suzanne Daley (NYT)

2000-06-23.title: Israel's Ruling Coalition Narrowly Averts a Breakup, Just as Mideast Peace Talks Near
2000-06-23.abstract: Shas party retracts its resignation from Israel's government after forcing leftist Meretz party out of cabinet; Prime Minister Ehud Barak, whose broad coalition is battered but intact as it head into a crucial stage of peacemaking with the Palestinians, appeals to nation to reflect and rise above recent internal turmoil, which he says threatens to sour everything that Israel has fought for and dreamed of; photo fo Shas leaders (M)

2000-06-23.title: Talks May Start Soon for South Koreans to Visit Relatives in North
2000-06-23.abstract: SEOUL—  For four days in November 1950, Sunwoo Hyun Bun walked, hitchhiked and rode railroad trains from Pyongyang, the North Korean capital, to South Korea.

2000-06-24.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-06-24.abstract: THE AMERICAS    COLOMBIA: KIDNAP VICTIMS FREED  The victims of a pair of politically emblematic kidnappings that deeply offended the Colombian public were both released. A right-wing paramilitary group freed Guillermo Valencia, left, a member of Congress who is the brother of one of the government's chief negotiators in peace talks with leftist guerrillas. He was seized Monday in an effort to torpedo the talks, while a 5-year-old girl whose parents said she was being held by the country's main left-wing guerrilla group was released after 70 days in captivity, but only after a ransom was paid.    Larry Rohter (NYT)

2000-06-24.title: Generation Gap Threatens Ruling Zimbabwe Party
2000-06-24.abstract: Dominance of Zimbabwe African National Union-Patriotic Front, party of Pres Robert Mugabe that has controlled country's politics for past 20 years, is threatened by generation gap; old message to remember past and to stay true to party that liberated country from white rule is being delivered to changing nation; about third of population consists of young people who were either children in 1980, when majority rule was achieved, or were born shortly after; significant percentage of 5.8 million voters have registered since February, after party suffered its first major political defeat; maps; photos (M)

2000-06-24.title: Polls Show Virtual Tie in the Crucial Presidential Race in Mexico
2000-06-24.abstract: Public opinion surveys show Francisco Labastida Ochoa, candidate of Mexico's governing Institutional Revolutionary Party, and Vicente Fox Quesada of center right National Action Party in virtual tie in presidential election, with as many as one-fourth of voters undecided (M)

2000-06-24.title: Shots Fired at TV Reporter in Mexico
2000-06-24.abstract: Shots are fired from high-caliber weapons at television reporter Lily Tellez in Mexico City in attack believed to be work of drug traffickers angered over her reports on narcotics trade; driver and bodyguards are hospitalized with bullet wounds; Tellez is unharmed S)

2000-06-24.title: Pentagon Sees China's Forces as No Foreseeable Threat to U.S.
2000-06-24.abstract: US Defense Dept report says China is modernizing its armed forces to counter military threats from technologically superior enemies, but that 'significant shortcomings' in its weapons and training will leave it unable to challenge US for indefinite period of time; its findings discussed (M)

2000-06-25.title: U.N. Warns That Trafficking In Human Beings Is Growing
2000-06-25.abstract: Pino Arlacchi, top anti-crime official at United Nations, says trafficking in people is now fastest-growing business of organized crime, and is being run by new, barely understood networks that have sidelined traditional criminal syndicates; cites recent deaths of 58 Chinese migrants trying to reach Britain as tip of iceberg; says estimated 200 million people may now be in some way or other under sway or in hands of traffickers of various kinds; he is considering recommending temporary residence to would-be immigrants who cooperate with authorities in identifying criminals who traffic in people (M)

2000-06-25.title: After Campaign Marked by Intimidation, Zimbabwe Voting Opens With Relative Calm
2000-06-25.abstract: First day of long-awaited national elections in Zimbabwe is reported largely peaceful; brazen intimidation and violence that marked campaign had threatened to mar vote seen as biggest challenge to grip of Pres Robert Mugabe in 20 years since white minority rule ended; country's more than five million voters are electing new Parliament; party that has controlled government since 1980 faces its first real challenge; photo; map (M)

2000-06-25.title: France Issues Tough Report On Monaco And Its Money
2000-06-25.abstract: French Parliament issues scathing 400-page report, accusing Monaco of deliberately putting in place lax banking laws to attract wealthy depositors no matter who they are; says monetary surveillance systems are so poor that even if Monaco officials want to cooperate in international efforts to prevent money laundering, they could not; report recommends that France review its accords with Monaco; says France, which supervises Monaco's lending agenices, could lose its own credibility in fight against money laundering (M)

2000-06-25.title: U.S. Sizes Up North Korea As a Partner In Business
2000-06-25.abstract: Clinton administration's easing of economic sanctions against North Korea prompts American corporations based in South Korea to assess business opportunities in country that has been out of bounds for five decades; North Korea, with 22 million people, represents unknown world for American business, with prospects large and small (M)

2000-06-25.title: The Six-Year Curse Threatens, but This Time Mexico Is Bullish
2000-06-25.abstract: Mexico has history of economic upheaval every six years, at end of every recent presdidential term; economists say that for once economy is strong enough to keep any disturbance caused by handover of power from becoming disaster; they credit Pres Ernesto Zedillo, himself an economist, who has spent last three years reshaping economy and building armor around country's finances to shield them from meltdown (M)

2000-06-26.title: Observers Issue Tough Report on Zimbabwe Vote Climate
2000-06-26.abstract: European Union obserevers issue harsh report on Zimbabwe's national elections, condemning government of Pres Robert Mugabe for supporting campaign of violence and intimidaton before vote and for obstructing work of thousands of local election monitors; comments by Pierre Schori, head of observer team, noted; photo (M)

2000-06-26.title: Conservatives In Canada Select Leader Of New Party
2000-06-26.abstract: Stockwell Day, 49-year-old flat-tax advocate and former treasurer of Alberta, emerges as frontrunner in race to lead Canadian Alliance, Canada's new conservative party; wins 44 percent of votes cast in four-way leadership primary, surpassing party's founder Preston Manning, who won 36 percent of vote; winner of July 8 runoff will face incumbent Prime Min Jean Chretien in election in 2001 (M)

2000-06-26.title: Anwar's Fate/Malaysian Judiciary at Issue : Explosive Trial Nears Its End
2000-06-26.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR—  After more than 100 courtroom sessions punctuated by heated arguments and shouting matches, the most politically explosive trial in Malaysia's modern history is close to a verdict.

2000-06-26.title: Elsie Widdowson, 93, a Pioneer in Nutrition
2000-06-26.abstract: Elsie Widdowson, nutritionist, dies at age 93; photo (M)

2000-06-26.title: Freetown Journal; The Walls of a Ruin Talk of History and Heartache
2000-06-26.abstract: City Hotel in Freetown, Sierre Leone, which Graham Greene immortalized into one of Africa's enduring literary landmarks in novel The Heart of the Matter and was quintessential artifact of British colonialism, is ravaged by fire; had survived end of British Empire, postcolonial slide into squalor and decay and rebel invasions that destroyed many surrounding buildings; photos (M)

2000-06-27.title: The 'Wondrous Map' of Gene Data : Historic Moment for Humanity's Blueprint
2000-06-27.abstract: LONDON—  The completion of a draft of the human genetic blueprint is a historic breakthrough that promises dramatic advances in the diagnosis and treatment of genetic defects, cancer and other illnesses, but the question of how to regulate this powerful information is likely to challenge society for years to come, scientists and politicians said Monday.

2000-06-27.title: Army Chief In Chechnya Is Reversed On Bombings
2000-06-27.abstract: Kremlin, in reversal of decision announced day ago, says attacks against rebels in Chechnya will continue (M)

2000-06-27.title: AMERICAN TOPICS : Even at Funerals, No Escaping Road Rage
2000-06-27.abstract: A funeral procession sometimes cannot drive peaceably through city streets without facing the sort of rudeness and rage that have poisoned parts of urban America.

2000-06-27.title: At Democracy's Picnic, Paris Supplies Ants
2000-06-27.abstract: France offers antidote to some of celebratory tone of conference on democracy in Warsaw, Poland, spearheaded by US; Foreign Min Hubert Vedrine says imposing democracy on country could have opposite result; cites instances in which US efforts to impose democracy by economnic sanctions or military force failed; US envoy Morton H Halperin says conference was not about 'export' of democracy but about enabling countries that had chosen democracy to help each other (M)

2000-06-27.title: Vera Atkins, 92, Spymaster for British, Dies
2000-06-27.abstract: Vera Atkins, who recruited, trained and watched over legendary British secret agents who parachuted into France to sabotage Nazis in World War II, dies at age 92; photo (M)

2000-06-28.title: Success of Zimbabwe's Opposition Signifies Big Changes
2000-06-28.abstract: Zimbabwe voters, in pointed rejection of Pres Robert Mugabe, take away his party's almost unchallenged authority in parliament by electing powerful new opposition party to more than third of seats; photo; map (M)

2000-06-28.title: World Briefing
2000-06-28.abstract: EUROPE    NORTHERN IRELAND: TROOP BUILDUP  Some 2,000 British troops are being flown in to increase security levels after the Parades Commission ruled that members of the militant Protestant Orange Order cannot follow a march path through a Catholic neighborhood after services at Drumcree parish next month. Similar increases were made in recent years before the so-called marching season, which has set off rioting across the province.    Warren Hoge (NYT)

2000-06-28.title: Assad's Son Designated Sole Candidate for Syrian Presidency
2000-06-28.abstract: Bashar al-Assad gets unanimous approval of Syrian parliament as sole presidential candidate in July 10 referendum, continuing swift march to power orchestrated by top aides as Hafez al-Assad slipped into final coma earlier this month (S)

2000-06-28.title: Italian Jews Denounce Vatican's Decision to Beatify Pius IX
2000-06-28.abstract: Italian Jews denounce Vatican decision to beatify Pius IX, who in 1858 abducted six-year-old Edgardo Mortara from his Jewish parents in Bologna and raised him as a Catholic; case became international cause celebre when pope adamantly refused to return child on grounds that he had secretly been baptized by illiterate Catholic servant; boy later became Catholic priest and died in Belgium in 1940; photo of pope (M)

2000-06-28.title: Clean Vote Vowed in Mexico, but Fraud Dies Hard
2000-06-28.abstract: Authorities promise that Mexico's presidential election will be cleanest ever, but recent reports of chicanery suggest that at least some hard-liners in governing PRI still believe they can manipulate outcome; incidents include bribe offers reported by polling officials in Tepeaca; photo; map; final polls show Francisco Labastida of PRI and Vicente Fox Quesada in dead heat (M)

2000-06-29.title: Minsk Journal; Sculpting a Monument to a Vanished Democracy
2000-06-29.abstract: Sculptor Ivan Y Misko plans to unveil bust of Gennadi Karpenko at late democracy advocate's tomb in rite that will be political act in authoritarian Belarus; Karpenko, until death in April 1999 under mysterious circumstances, led forces seeking to oust Pres Aleksandr G Lukashenko; Karpenko's wife says family, harassed by security forces, will leave nation after rite; photo (M)

2000-06-29.title: Cuban-Americans in Miami Bitter Over Boy's Return
2000-06-29.abstract: Cuban-Americans gather outside home of Elian Gonzalez's Miami relatives to watch on television boy's departure for Cuba; deplore his return (M)

2000-06-29.title: 40 China Experts Ask Delay on Antimissile Plan
2000-06-29.abstract: Over 40 American scholars on China and former diplomats are urging Pres Clinton to delay decision on whether to build national missile defense, warning of negative impact on ties with China; Council for a Livable World gathered their signaturees on letter to Clinton (M)

2000-06-29.title: Justices Allow Cuban Boy to Fly Home
2000-06-29.abstract: Elian Gonzalez, 6, returns to Cuba with father and other friends and relatives after Supreme Court refuses to hear arguments in asylum bid for the six-year-old by his Miami relatives; is greeted at airport in Havana by grandparents and hundreds of flag-waving students hurriedly bused in from his home town of Cardenas; Cuban officials do not plan any news conferences or rallies for boy, who is met by National Assembly Pres Ricardo Alarcon, who guided drive for his return; photos (M)

2000-06-29.title: Hanna Batatu, 74, Authority On Politics of Iraq and Syria
2000-06-29.abstract: Dr Hanna Batatu, expert on contemporary Arab world, dies at age 74; photo (M)

2000-06-30.title: Kohl Remains Defiant in Testimony to Parliament Inquiry
2000-06-30.abstract: Former German Chancellor Helmut Kohl appears before parliamentary committee investigating financial misdeeds under his rule, saying he is victim of slander; stance is consistent with defiance Kohl, who was chancellor from 1982 to 19988, has shown since he admitted in December that he had taken about $1 million in undeclared and illicit payments from donors and used it to boster his Christian Democratic Party in formerly Communist eastern Germany; photo (M)

2000-06-30.title: THE NUCLEAR SHIELD: Repelling an Attack; A Missile Defense With Limits: The ABC's of the Clinton Plan
2000-06-30.abstract: Special report, The Nuclear Shield, describes in detail the missile defense system envisioned by Clinton adminstration and its military advisers; Pentago officials and some experts outside government say defense technology has reached new level of maturity, with combination of miniaturized parts and fast rockets that can produce an agile, lightweight weapon capable of finding and ramming an incoming warhead at about 7,000 miles per hour; critics question whether highly complex weapon can survive boost into orbit and whether it can be made to distinguish between attacking warheads and decoys; in fact, since research on so-called hit-to-kill weapons began in 1976, attempts to destroy mock warheads have failed more than 70 percent of time; success of crucial upcoming flight test, in when a ground-based interceptor missile will try to destroy a mock warhead in space, could lead to decision by Pres Clinton or next administration to begin construction of first phase of national missile defense; central feature of system would be a supersmart 'exoatmospheric kill vehicle' designed to be carried atop a rocket and guide itself to collision with incoming warhead; charts, diagram and map show how system is designed to work (L)

2000-06-30.title: After Boy's Return, Castro Tries to Keep Momentum
2000-06-30.abstract: Cuban government is keeping Elian Gonzalez in the news and continuing public rallies in apparent attempt to tap into overwhelming support for boy's return to rekindle some of revolutionary ardor that has cooled in recent years and to restore appeal of Pres Fidel Castro (M)

2000-06-30.title: Women Become the Darlings Of the Candidates in Mexico
2000-06-30.abstract: Mexico's political parties are courting women voters as never before in this year's presidential election; with statistical dead heat between governing party candidate Francisco Labastida Ocho and center-right opponent Vicente Fox Quesada, women's votes may be decisive; some 52 percent of Mexican electorate are women, and more than a third of all voters are homemakers; the courting of women's vote is also changing focus of campaigns, with questions being asked about both candidates' divorces, a subject long considered taboo for public discussion; unusually high-profile roles of political wives and daughters this year signals new opening for women and women's issues in government; photos (M)

2000-06-30.title: U.S. Gains a Compromise On War Crimes Tribunal
2000-06-30.abstract: United States wins agreement to keep door open to further talks on its demand that no American official or soldier be surrendered to world's first permanent court for war crimes as long as US has not signed treaty creating International Criminal Court (M)
